programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Father Dowling Mysteries
programme.desc: Part two of two. The crime-busting cleric finds himself caught up in a strange mystery as he attempts to prove a fellow priest innocent of murder. But with the real killer now dead, he must try to establish a connection with the Mafia. Tom Bosley and Tracy Nelson star

programme.title: Father Dowling Mysteries
programme.desc: The crime-solving clergyman's evil half-brother appears on the scene, with his eye on some precious gems. Unfortunately, the police are unable to tell the pair apart. Drama, starring Tom Bosley, Tracy Nelson and Richard Beale

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: In Washington, Kimble gets the protection of an African Ambassador but incurs the hostility of his wife

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Five beautiful women, including an amnesia victim who is Perry Mason's client, turn up as suspects in a murder case

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker is haunted by his fiancee's memory when her killer is released from prison. Adventure with the modern-day lawman whose crime-solving methods are rooted firmly in the Old West. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Horatio tries to track down the killer of a model intentionally run down outside a hotel, and manages to identify a suspect, but government red tape hampers the case. Meanwhile, Yelina struggles to get a grip on her romantic feelings. Drama following the work of a forensic investigation unit, starring David Caruso and Sofia Milos

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A serial killer begins posting footage of his crimes online - and as the agents investigate, they realise that each film contains visual clues to the identity of the next victim. In a further bizarre twist, Abby later finds what seems to be a new video on her computer - and the case takes on fresh urgency with the discovery that it is actually a live feed, suggesting the murderer is somewhere on the premises

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Sam agrees to help an old friend whose nephew has been kidnapped, and although Granger and Hetty forbid him from investigating the case in company time, they soon change their minds when they discover the culprit is Haakim 'the Ambassador' Hamami, a wanted terrorist with connections to the Taliban. LL Cool J, Chris O'Donnell, Linda Hunt and Miguel Ferrer star

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble falls in love with a woman who, unknown to him, is a daytime parolee from a woman's prison

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry Mason must determine whether word of a planned business merger was leaked through the letters of two little girls

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team flies to Israel, but receives a hostile reception from Ziva's father, who is the head of intelligence agency Mossad. As events unfold, Gibbs learns that Ziva was romantically involved with Rivkin and finds himself forced to make a tough decision. Guest starring Michael Nouri, alongside Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: LaRue manages to disarm the bailiffs during his trial, demands to see Walker and taunts Alex while he waits

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Confidential files are leaked by an anonymous hacker, who threatens to release more unless the Navy agrees to renew a contract with a struggling shipbuilding firm. The Navy Secretary suspects the company's owner Philip Wickes is to blame, and asks NCIS to join forces with the PsyOps unit in an elaborate ruse to bring the culprit out of hiding. Meanwhile, Gibbs' colleagues begin to notice his attraction to his opposite number Samantha Ryan. Guest starring Jamie Lee Curtis

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Sam's wife Michelle resumes her CIA cover to investigate a hit on a Russian mob boss with possible links to Sidorov and the stolen nukes. Her husband is understandably unhappy about her involvement - and it seems his worries are justified when concerns grow for her safety. LL Cool J and Chris O'Donnell star, with Aunjanue Ellis, Daniela Ruah and Barrett Foa

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony travels to North Africa on the trail of missing agent Ziva, who is thought to have been tracking down a terrorist leader called Saleem Ulman. Intent on exacting revenge for her apparent death during a flight to Somalia, he is taken hostage by militants and discovers she is still alive - only to be given a potentially catastrophic ultimatum. Starring Mark Harmon and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: Holby City

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Harry prepares to say goodbye to the hospital and move on to his new career as an MP, but his final shift is marred by the death of a pensioner and a seemingly insincere farewell speech by Nathan. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Arun is incensed when a gang on the Bronte estate sets upon a young couple. He is then completely taken in by one of the local youngsters, who draws him into a trap that strips him of his dignity - and most of his uniform. Smithy sets out to trace the gang by way of Arun's stolen radio, and the crusading PC is shocked to discover who is masterminding the beatings and muggings - and is all the more determined to nail them this time

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Tony is horrified when he is confronted over his relationship with Polly. A delighted Barry decides that it does pay to mix business with pleasure when Vanessa visits him

programme.desc: Bianca bemoans the lack of a decent birthday present from Ricky. Ted oversleeps and misses his lift to work. Anita Banks comes to look at Mark and Ruth's house

She refuses to answer his questions and is unwilling to be drawn into a defence of her character because she feels she is the one who has had her privacy invaded. However, Jack persists in demanding an explanation, causing her to do the one thing he never expected. Australian drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detective investigates when former war photographer Peter Rooker is found shot dead. It is not long before he uncovers a trail leading from tabloid newspaper baron Eddie Price and editor Melissa Booth to an executed Bosnian family, but when he enlists his wife's help in determining the psychological history behind the murder, a fatal chain of events begins

With guest appearances by Roger Lloyd Pack and George Costigan

programme.desc: A body found hanging in the children's playground of a recently regenerated model community proves to be that of a member of the local Community Watch scheme. Suspicion falls on the chairman of the organisation Harry Wallace, a founding father who helped clean up the estate for the residents. Reid fights to clear the local hero's name, but her colleagues are not so easily impressed when it emerges he has form for accidentally killing a man. Glasgow-set detective drama, guest starring Phil McKee, with Blythe Duff

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: The opening of Hugh's new cardiac clinic is upset by life-changing news from his ex-wife, and Charlie's life is thrown into disarray when her father makes a visit

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Meryl and Carlito's relationship leaves the Knight family reeling, the tension between Hugh and Penny endangers the new cardiac clinic, and Matt reconnects with a high school crush

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: A drug dealer dies while in police custody and Sam suspects a cover-up within the unit. He orders a full investigation, though it seems none of the other officers is prepared to help. To Gene's relief, the pathologist's report appears to put everyone in the clear, but when the truth about the criminal's demise emerges, it is up to the 21st-century cop to decide whether to reveal it. programme.desc: The team reopens a case originally worked on by a corrupt colleague of Sandra's father, leading the detective to seek the truth about her family's shady past - a decision that puts pressure on the relationship with her mother. programme.desc: The team is drawn into the world of a strict religious sect when a father and son are murdered with copies of the Bible at their sides. Burke grows increasingly irate as the secretive community closes ranks, refusing to speak to non-members, and he suspects the leader James Hardie knows more about the killings than he is letting on. Meanwhile, Reid investigates the disappearance of an old friend's daughter and uncovers a link to Burke's case. Detective drama, guest starring Sandy Morton, with Alex Norton and Blythe Duff

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: There are embarrassing results when Penny's personal feelings for Hugh spill over into the workplace, while Charlie and Matt attempt to cope with an unwanted house guest

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Penny gives Hugh an ultimatum about their relationship, while Meryl and Betty land Hugh as their star attraction at the first Whyhope speed dating night

programme.title: Life on Mars

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Siegfried is pleased with his new time-management system - until he misplaces his diary and has an unfortunate encounter with a Great Dane. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy and Robert Hardy

programme.desc: Ajax takes desperate measures to stop Hugh selling the farm, Penny discovers her perfect match thanks to Meryl's dating service, and Matt gets drunk with April

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The reds and blue teams go shopping in the antique centres of Hungerford, Berkshire, joined by experts Caroline Hawley and David Harper. Eric Knowles presents and assesses their purchases

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Karen talks to the dead, while Sid thinks drinking is the answer. Emma and Harriet encounter two women battling with grief

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Contestants Hannah, Elayne, Rupesh and Greg vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to win a cash prize. Quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton goes property shopping in Exmoor with newlyweds who have a £625 000 budget for a home that will house his parents and provide her with land for horses

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers make over an empty plot in the Oxfordshire town of Wantage. Grace and her mother Agnes want a contemporary design with a nod toward Zimbabwe, with exotic planting and a Victoria Falls-inspired water feature

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and Phil Serrell go on the hunt for more elusive antique treasures. A gamble on some brown furniture may prove profitable at the Hertfordshire auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Having chased Kat's mugger, Callum blames himself when the man gets away by attacking Fitzy, and cannot open up to Ben about what happened. Kat is reluctant to take Phil up on his business offer, not wanting to get involved in anything dodgy. Ruby and Lily reach and agreement and Stuart is appalled when Vi tries to set him up with Honey

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Callum is snappy with Ben, insisting he is fine despite being anything but - and things only get worse when Whitney and Kat ask him about the stabbing. Rainie is suspicious of Vi's sudden nice act, forcing Stuart to admit she knows about their surrogacy plans, Lola worries about Isaac when he lies about his medication, and Linda realises she will have to tell Max about the baby

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Kian discovers that Lucky betrayed him by calling social services, and as plans begin to move Andrei away, a desperate and powerless Kian spirals out of control. Ange and Josh are questioned about their secret relationship by Hanssen, leaving them worrying about their future at Holby, and Dom feels rejected when a patient requests Sacha to be her consultant

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metal worker Dominic Chinea takes on a former fairground family's water can, while Suzie Fletcher tackles a miniature camel saddle - the only memento from a childhood spent in Malaysia. This curious item had been safely by owner John's side for 60 years, until an unfortunate incident with a mischievous puppy left it in tatters. Teddy bear repair duo Amanda and Julie welcome a 100-year-old RAF mascot bear called Bobby

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The three finalists take part in their biggest professional assignment to date, delivering their own masterclass in which they demonstrate a signature look in front of a panel of industry heavyweights. One contestant tackles male grooming, the second showcases special effects and the third aims to dazzle with a quick smokey eye. The next day the trio face their final creative brief, coming up with a look that demonstrates what makes them unstoppable as a make-up artist, before the judges decide which two will compete in the final face off. Hosted by Maya Jama

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: Lockdown is finally lifted, and in Bridgend Scott gets to open his doughnuts and coffee cafe. Cardiff nightclub owner Panikos auditions Russian cook Shev for the role of head chef, while vintage clothing entrepreneur and keyboard player Nanial prepares for his band to launch their first album

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: The Psycho prequel series returns as Norma sets out in search of the missing Norman. Meanwhile, Dylan awaits news of Emma, and Romero receives a surprising proposition. Thriller, starring Freddie Highmore and Vera Farmiga

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norma and Norman find themselves at odds, as each suspects the other of committing horrible actions. Meanwhile, Dylan considers his future with Emma, and Romero makes a decision that will have a big impact on the Bates family. Thriller, starring Vera Farmiga and Freddie Highmore

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow ambitious property development projects in Brynmawr in south Wales, Upchurch in Kent and Stone in Staffordshire. All the projects take longer than planned, with one stretching over four years

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Ben and Myles visit an abandoned hotel in search of money owed to a former employee, and the Sheriffs come up against a car dealer who is refusing to refund a customer

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a discarded scrap of paper found in a household bin helped convict a killer, and a distinctive mole holds the key to tracking down a dangerous and predatory attacker

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Richard Madley and Jonathan Pratt give advice to two teams at Wetherby racecourse. Plus, host Charlie Ross heads to the Royal Armouries in Leeds to learn about animal armour

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Sid finds himself in trouble with the rapid response team, while Emma and Jimmi worry about Rob. A young student turns to Al for help when she falls victim to bullying

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz in which contestants aim to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest. Battling it out today are Liam, Sam, Sarah and Kiya

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to the East Midlands to help a couple find a comfortable country home that brings them closer to family for £450,000. While in the region, Jonnie visits the Leicester Line of the Grand Union canal, a stretch where narrow boats have to navigate a total of 59 locks. He tries his hand at lock keeper's duties, including operating the floodgates

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Ellesmere Port, James wants to turn his neglected garden into a Cornish coastal haven, providing a safe space for his dog and visiting family. Enter Charlie Dimmock and Lee Burkhill with competing designs. Which one will James decide to go with?

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and Phil Serrell drive through a downpour in Oxfordshire on the trail of treasures, pinning their hopes on a long-lost saucer and a designer brooch

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Former wheelchair racer Richie Powell investigates the sport's classification system, asking whether something designed to make competition between athletes with different disabilities fair is flawed, easily manipulated and lacking credibility

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie, Harry and David visit Ibstock, Leicestershire, where they meet policeman Norman and Piper, who works in intensive care. They need a garden they can relax and unwind in - somewhere that barbecue-mad Norman can grill and chill, while Piper, who enjoys baking, can grow plants to add fragrance and flavour to her cakes. They have £4,000 to spend, but whose brief will they prefer?

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett hosts the semi-final, as the four remaining sewers go back in time to the 1940s. Attempting to revive both the glamorous and thrifty styles of the decade, they tackle the decade's most elegant and innovative garments. First up, judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young challenge the sewers to make the decade's most famous trousers, Oxford bags. For the Transformation Challenge, the sewers are set a wartime task to turn old parachutes into glamorous dresses. In the Made to Measure challenge, the sewers take on their final icon of 40s fashion, Dior's New Look. Characterised by an hourglass silhouette and involving masses of fabric, the sewers must exaggerate their model's figure to create the iconic shape

programme.title: Krept and Konan: We Are England
programme.desc: Documentary following the rap duo as they set out to make the England anthem for the Euro 2020 tournament. Along the way they get to know the squad, meeting players including Everton's Dominic Calvert-Lewin, Aston Villa's Tyrone Mings and West Ham's Declan Rice, and ask manager Gareth Southgate which ingredients he would like in the final song. They also seek advice from fellow artists Aitch and Big Zuu

programme.title: Football, Racism and Social Media
programme.desc: A look at the four days when the footballing world, with support from across sport, used its voice by staying silent on social media. The boycott was the latest attempt at tackling the racist abuse suffered by black and Asian players. Yet even those at the centre of the campaign conceded little would change as a result. Nesta McGregor speaks to victims, social media companies and those charged with cleaning up the sport to investigate why the abuse is happening and what can be done to stop it

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell track the progress of terraced houses in Grassmoor near Chesterfield, Queenborough in Sheerness, Kent, and St Helens on Merseyside

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: The Sheriffs are tasked with removing squatters from a shop in north London. James and Mark are in Birmingham hoping to takeaway money apparently owed by a kebab shop, and the Sheriffs visit a garage that allegedly damaged the wheels of a customer's pride and joy

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a single glove uncovered the identities of a prolific and violent gang, and an image of a distinctive ponytail that solved a high-tech bank heist

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from Ballinderry in Northern Ireland, where experts Ben Cooper and Charles Hanson help two teams spend £300 on three items that they hope will make a profit at auction. Anita also pays a visit to one of Belfast's well known watering holes, the Crown Bar pub

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Sid plans an ambitious initiative for the Mill, and Al is less than helpful in Bear's audit meeting. Zara is forced to intervene between a mother and her stroppy teenage daughter

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz in which contestants aim to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest. Battling it out today are Sam, Sharna, Gary and Sandy

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps two brothers search for a house in Herefordshire. The duo have pooled their resources to have £800,000 to find the right country home where they can live together. Away from the hunt, Alistair is given an exclusive invitation to experience the best in British agriculture at the Royal Three Counties Show

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Ben and Shelley's new-build garden in Wing, Buckinghamshire, is sloped, impractical and has a tiny patio. Having travelled extensively across Europe, the couple yearn for a Mediterranean courtyard-style garden to remind them of their adventures abroad. It must also feature a children's play zone for youngsters Eva and Maya. Siblings David and Harry Rich Brothers vie with Arit Anderson to help the pair transform this troublesome space

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Izzie Balmer hit the shops of Staffordshire and Shropshire. Izzie's new obsession turns out to be serpentine in nature as she gets her hands on a couple of snake-related items. For Phil, there's a rather special bit of jewellery from a big-name designer that he has his sights on

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the magazine show, featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bobby arrives at the restaurant for another date with Dana, only for Iqra to drop him in it by suggesting that he should cook them dinner. Rainie tells Vi to go back home - only to be caught off guard when the old busybody lets her in on a secret. Nancy encourages Mick to speak up about Linda's plan to get in touch with Max about the baby. Isaac continues digging for information on Paul and Callum digs himself deeper as Ben makes a discovery

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies are joined by former Dragon Theo Paphitis, who standing in for a self-isolating Peter Jones. The Dragons are served a selection of premium spiced rums by a confident drinks entrepreneur. A nursing bra designed to help mothers and babies breastfeed effectively and a heatless hair curling device invented in a garden shed are also offered up, while a Midlands-based duo pitch a voice-enhanced app for learner-readers. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Launch Show
programme.desc: Gary Lineker and guests look ahead to this summer's European Championship, which begins in Rome with the curtain-raiser between Turkey and Italy. The tournament, which was delayed by a year because of the coronavirus pandemic, will take place in 11 different cities across the continent, with the semi-finals and final staged at London's Wembley Stadium. England have only ever won one knockout tie at the Euros, but hopes are high Gareth Southgate's side can improve that record. The Three Lions, who boast a formidable array of attacking options, begin their campaign against Croatia on Sunday. Scotland meet the Czech Republic in their opener, while Wales face Switzerland. Gary is joined by former England defender Micah Richards, ex-Scotland star James McFadden and Ashley Williams, who captained Wales at Euro 2016

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Our Cops in the North
programme.desc: A fire that kills a man and could have led to more deaths appears to be arson and the culprit is likely to strike again - but identifying the criminal proves tricky as all the evidence was destroyed in the blaze. Meanwhile, a neighbourhood team gathers enough intelligence to raid the address of a local suspected drug dealer, and paramedics are prevented from carrying out CPR

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and Philip Serrell scour the shops of Kent for antiques, indulging in some rocking horse riding, cat whispering and disagreements about scarf wearing on the way

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers come up with plans for a wildlife-friendly Victorian cottage garden for two retired teachers who have bought a home in Oxford

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don gives advice on plants to use for foliage with tropical proportions. Plus, Arit Anderson continues investigating the use of peat, and Carol Klein explores Oxford Botanic Garden's euphorbias

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc in which pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords as they attempt to take home the £2,500 prize money

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin teaches would-be antiques dealers the tricks of the trade, pitching Pauline Oldford from Telford against Peter Gibbons from Bedfordshire, before an auction showdown

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three Scotland chefs hope to impress with their main dishes. Angela Malik prepares a tiffin box featuring a summer biriyani, quail scotch egg and spiced quail breasts, while Michael Bremner takes a huge risk by using ox tongue as the centrepiece of his main. Ally McGrath devises an inventive take on the summer barbecue, using a new method of cooking potatoes to recreate the coals

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chefs Angela Malik, Ally McGrath and Michael Bremner continue their battle to cook at a Taste of Summer banquet celebrating 140 years of the Wimbledon Championships. As each of them prepare their desserts, they will all be out to impress as only two can make it through to tomorrow's Scotland final

programme.title: Bees, Butterflies and Blooms
programme.desc: Sarah Raven encourages gardeners in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, to embrace flowers rich in pollen and nectar over traditional bedding plants, as she continues her mission to halt the decline in bee, butterfly and pollinating insect populations. She also launches a bid for a pollinator-friendly logo to be introduced at the Chelsea Flower Show, and finds out from naturalist Steve Head how her own garden can be improved

programme.title: Nature's Microworlds
programme.desc: Steve Backshall examines life in Australia's Red Centre, a desert landscape in the Outback, where two-metre tall kangaroos, burrowing toads and the world's most venomous snakes are among the throng of animals that live there. He also looks at how, in a place that is parched by the sun, scorched by fire and prone to unpredictable floods, creatures successfully make the heart of this island continent their home

programme.title: Reel History of Britain
programme.desc: Melvyn Bragg heads to Countesthorpe in Leicestershire to take a look back at the Queen's Silver Jubilee celebrations of 1977. Jayne Taylor and Linda Leeke recall the day they travelled to London and ended up in the news, royal biographer Hugo Vickers recounts his role in the festivities, and former beauty queen Nicky Grossman sees footage of her younger self

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Catherine Southon and Mark Stacey value items and collectibles brought in by the public to Lulworth Castle in Dorset. Plus, presenter Paul Martin explores the turbulent history of the Weld family and the anti-Catholic persecution they were subject to for more than 300 years

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon, Clive Myrie, Sara Pascoe and Rick Edwards test their general knowledge skills in more trivia-based games, with the winner announced on Friday

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo visits Cornwall's county town, Truro, heading to the historic estate of Trewithen, whose gardens were stocked from China by professional plant hunters commissioned by its owner. The Atlantic Coast branch line carries Michael north to Newquay, where he discovers a pioneering surfer and braves the waves on a belly board. In the nearby village of St Mawgan, Michael is introduced to the ancient Cornish sport of 'wrassling', which surged in popularity between the wars as part of a Cornish Celtic revival

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Daniel Brocklebank and Hayley Tamaddon search for antiques in East Anglia, taking a trip to Great Yarmouth's Pleasure Beach and discovering a scheme to rehouse unwanted dogs

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan bring live updates from Norfolk, and Gillian Burke investigates some clever cuttlefish in Northern Ireland. Meanwhile, Iolo Williams tracks down mountain hares in the Highlands, and sea slugs making a colourful appearance

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: As the Covid-19 lockdown beings to ease, the Emergency Department at University Hospital, Coventry experiences an increase in patients presenting mental health concerns. Faced with cases of self-harm and suicide, NHS staff must deal with a dwindling number of mental health beds when trying to give patients the care they need

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Guests Jo Brand, Clive Anderson and Jimmy Carr join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'new' in this extended episode. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Managing England: The Impossible Job
programme.desc: A look at the history of the role of England football manager, featuring interviews with current boss Gareth Southgate and former incumbents Sven-Goran Eriksson, Roy Hodgson and Sam Allardyce. Past players such as Gary Lineker, Alan Shearer and Rio Ferdinand also contribute as the programme looks at why it is such a difficult job and how the nation's trophy drought in the sport can be ended

programme.title: The Road to Partition
programme.desc: Part two of two. On June 22, 1921, King George V and Queen Mary arrived in Belfast for the official opening of the first parliament of Northern Ireland. The King's visit to Belfast was the culmination of three centuries of history. This concluding film examines how political tensions and outbreaks of sectarian violence threatened to destabilise the new Northern Irish state

programme.title: What Are We Feeding Our Kids?
programme.desc: Across the world childhood obesity rates have risen tenfold in 50 years. In the UK 21 per cent of children are living with obesity when they leave primary school - the highest it's ever been. Ultra-processed food is cheap and convenient, and accounts for the majority of young people's calorie intake - 64 per cent for children and 67 per cent for teenagers. Yet alarmingly, scientists know very little about exactly how it affects youngsters' bodies. Dr Chris van Tulleken investigates the impact of ultra-processed food, as he undergoes a gruelling self-experiment that even shocks the scientists

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood joins everyday hard grafters who keep the country's essential infrastructure running, beginning in Belfast harbour, where ferries leave for the UK mainland. His mentor is operations manager Howard Hillis, who has worked at the harbour for the last 21 years. Dom is thrown in at the deep end, spending 24 hours at sea, learning the ropes alongside the captain and many of his crew, before transferring to the historic Harland & Wolff dockyards, where the Titanic was built, to assist with the essential maintenance of a ship in dry dock

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan are broadcasting live from Wild Ken Hill in Norfolk, as cameras capture the dramas of such birds as avocets and barn owls. Elsewhere, Iolo Williams encounters some red deer at Alladale Wilderness Reserve in Scotland, and Gillian Burke dips into the vast waters of Strangford Lough in Northern Ireland

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and Phil Serrell go on the hunt for more elusive antique treasures. A gamble on some brown furniture may prove profitable at the Hertfordshire auction

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers make over an empty plot in the Oxfordshire town of Wantage. Grace and her mother Agnes want a contemporary design with a nod toward Zimbabwe, with exotic planting and a Victoria Falls-inspired water feature

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A London-based entrepreneur with an assortment of naturally derived sugar alternatives hopes he can pull off a sweet deal, while a couple face a roasting over their optimistic projections for their vitamin-infused coffee. Multimillionaires Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies are also approached by an inventor who hopes to fix a deal with her portable children's suction mats and consider investing in a business selling innovative eco-cleaning products. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc in which pairs of contestants go head to head to crack a series of passwords as they attempt to take home the £2,500 prize money

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: In Derbyshire, Paul Martin teaches would-be antiques dealers the tricks of the trade, pitching Zoe Taylor from the Midlands against Stephen Hylands from Belfast. An auction showdown later reveals who has made the largest profit and made the highest percentage of profit

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Scotland chefs compete for a place in the national finals, preparing their entire menus for judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort, Andi Oliver and guest Gary Parsons - executive chef at Wimbledon - as the programme continues its tennis-themed series

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The entrants representing Wales prepare starters, with returning chef Phil Carmichael taking on newcomers Paul Croasdale and Nick Brodie. The three chefs all create complex dishes, with both Paul and Phil championing the tomato, while Nick hopes his rabbit and carrot dish will give him the edge over the other two

programme.title: Bees, Butterflies and Blooms
programme.desc: Sarah Raven travels to Birmingham, where she begins her efforts to create more habitats for pollinating insects in cities by challenging the parks department to replace its acres of close-mown grass and Victorian bedding displays with modern, sustainable alternatives. She then heads to the London Olympic Park to see the tens of thousands of pollinator-friendly meadows that are being sown to come into flower in time for this summer's Games, and tests seed mixes in her own garden

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan bring live updates from Norfolk, and Gillian Burke investigates some clever cuttlefish in Northern Ireland. Meanwhile, Iolo Williams tracks down mountain hares in the Highlands, and sea slugs making a colourful appearance

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and experts Christina Trevanion and Will Axon travel to the Oxford Union, where they pick out antiques and collectibles to be sold at a local auction - including an autograph book and some etchings by a famous artist. Plus, the presenter goes behind the scenes at the venue, to discover which famous faces debated in the chamber

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon, Clive Myrie, Sara Pascoe and Rick Edwards continue to test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches Bodmin en route to Totnes, as he explores the West Country from St Ives to Salisbury Plain. Out on the rugged moor, Michael hears how the celebrated author Daphne du Maurier captivated readers between the wars with her tales of smuggling at the Jamaica Inn. In Devon, Michael takes the plunge at Plymouth's beautiful art deco Tinside Lido, while at Ivybridge, he boards a vehicle like no other to cross the causeway to Burgh Island, where a 1930s playboy built a splendid art deco party palace

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Boy band legends Brian McFadden and Keith Duffy hunt for antiques in the dales of Derbyshire. It is a Westlife versus Boyzone battle as Keith teams up with expert Roo Irvine and Brian gets up close and personal with Margie Cooper. Keith plies the Irish charm to secure a discount on a George III silver fruit spoon, while Brian uncovers some Victorian opera glasses that gets his expert quite excited. Between the shopping, Brian and Margie get a brief history of time at a traditional clockmakers, while Keith needs to call in a mountain rescue team's search dog to find a missing Roo. They then head to auction in Sheffield

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan hope to see the rare turtle dove at Wild Ken Hill in Norfolk, while Gillian Burke gets close to young Manx shearwaters in Northern Ireland and Iolo Williams ducks into the river at Alladale to look for lampreys

programme.title: Building Britain's Biggest Nuclear Power Station
programme.desc: Following the challenges involved with building the Hinkley Point C in the remote Somerset countryside, the first new nuclear power station in Britain for a generation. This time, the team create an extensive flood defence system to protect the site from a nuclear disaster like the one that befell the Fukushima Daiichi power plant in Japan. Elsewhere, cameras follow specialists as they work to uncover unexploded Second World War bombs that threaten the installation of the 4500-tonne cooling water intake heads

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Leading performers on the stand-up circuit share their light-hearted views on modern life in this show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. Tonight, the host Henning Wehn welcomes Lucy Porter and Guz Khan to the stage

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: As the Covid-19 lockdown beings to ease, the Emergency Department at University Hospital, Coventry experiences an increase in patients presenting mental health concerns. Faced with cases of self-harm and suicide, NHS staff must deal with a dwindling number of mental health beds when trying to give patients the care they need

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Kirsty joins Carole and Mairi at Beechgrove as they explore sustainable hanging basket options, including an upcycled bird cage. Brian reveals the next stage of his no dig converted lawn at Old Scone

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: Mary Beard talks to James May, Gary Numan, David Olusoga, Rosie Jones and William Dalrymple to explore what people lose from arts and culture when they cannot travel. In London's St Pancras station, she asks if it is architecture and art that give train station reunions their romantic edge.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores East Anglia between the wars, beginning at Sutton Hoo in Suffolk, where archaeologists discovered a 27-metre-long Anglo-Saxon ship in 1939. In the Essex village of Dedham, he unearths a nasty brush between painters, with art experts explaining the antipathy between traditional artist Alfred Munnings and the modern art school established in the village by Cedric Morris

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Gillian Burke follows the fortunes of a harbour seal in hospital in Northern Ireland, while Iolo Williams is on the look out for golden eagles in the Glen. Elsewhere, Michaela Strachan and Chris Packham assess the effects of this year's late spring on our wildlife

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and Phil Serrell drive through a downpour in Oxfordshire on the trail of treasures, pinning their hopes on a long-lost saucer and a designer brooch

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Ellesmere Port, James wants to turn his neglected garden into a Cornish coastal haven, providing a safe space for his dog and visiting family. Enter Charlie Dimmock and Lee Burkhill with competing designs. Which one will James decide to go with?

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon returns with the property makeover show in which cutting-edge virtual reality and visual effects enable people to see what the future of their home could look like, before it is built in reality. In the first edition architects Laura Jane Clark and Julian McIntosh compete to remodel Shelley and Steven's three-bedroom semi-detached home in Bromley, south-east London

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc in which contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords as they attempt to take home the £2,500 prize money

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Katie Jeffryes and Noman Hussein from Essex go up against each other as Paul Martin shows them the tricks of the antiques trade before they face an auction showdown

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Pressure in the kitchen is high as the chefs prepare their fish courses. Paul Croasdale is trying to recreate the elegance of Wimbledon with luxury ingredients such as king crab and scallops. Meanwhile, Phil Carmichael and Nick Brodie are both preparing meals using mackerel which hope they can elevate for the exacting standards of the All England Club

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The entrants representing Wales, returning chef Phil Carmichael and newcomers Paul Croasdale and Nick Brodie, go all out to impress with their main courses. Each chef uses Welsh lamb as the centrepiece of their dish, showcasing a range of techniques and using an array of unusual ingredients to elevate their main courses to banquet level

programme.title: Sicily: Wonder of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Part one of two. Michael Scott traces the history of Sicily, from the first arrival of ancient Greek settlers 3,000 years ago to the arrival of the Byzantine Empire. He begins his journey on the slopes of Mount Etna, Europe's largest active volcano, and discovers how the Greeks began to settle on Sicily's east coast - planting their olives and vines and building great city states that soon came to rival even Athens itself

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan hope to see the rare turtle dove at Wild Ken Hill in Norfolk, while Gillian Burke gets close to young Manx shearwaters in Northern Ireland and Iolo Williams ducks into the river at Alladale to look for lampreys

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Thomas Plant and Charlie Ross evaluate the public's antiques at the Fleet Air Arm Museum, situated on the Navy's largest aviation base, HMS Heron in Somerset. A surprise discovery by the venue's director pushes the value up on one item, while presenter Paul Martin meets curator Dave Morris, whose pioneering way of working is bringing the exhibits to life in a way never previously imagined

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Comedian Sara Pascoe, news anchor Clive Myrie, and TV presenters Angela Scanlon and Rick Edwards continue the daily test of their general knowledge skills. Hosted by Richard Osman

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's exploration of the West Country continues in south Devon. He arrives in Paignton to investigate an extraordinarily high murder rate in the literature of the 1930s. The Dartmouth Steam Railway conveys him in style to the beautiful home of Agatha Christie, in the company of her great-grandson, James Prichard

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England V New Zealand. Action from day one of the series-concluding Second Test at Edgbaston. England's last Test at this venue was in 2019, when they lost to Australia by 251 runs, denying them a fifth straight victory here and ending a eight-match unbeaten run

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan investigate how British hedgehogs are coping this spring, and Megan McCubbin joins them live in Norfolk to discuss efforts to save the corncrake. Iolo Williams is in the Scottish Highlands, checking on some very special wildcats, and Gillian Burke has a fishy tale about flapper skates in Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Statue Wars: One Summer in Bristol
programme.desc: On Sunday, June 7, 2020, protestors marching to support the Black Lives Matter movement tore down the statue of slave trader Edward Colston and threw it in Bristol's harbour. Caught in the eye of this storm was Bristol's mayor Marvin Rees, the first directly elected mayor of Black African heritage of a major European city. Born and bred in Bristol and himself a descendant of enslaved people, how would he hold the city together in the face of rising tensions that threaten to explode into violent confrontation?

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Hugh Dennis run through the main talking points of the last seven days with the support of guests Ed Byrne, Rhys James, Athena Kugblenu, Laura Lexx and Alasdair Beckett-King

programme.title: Festival of Funny: Shorts
programme.desc: King of the court Carl is forced to question his idea of masculinity after an obsession with a mysterious new member of his badminton club spirals out of control. Tom Greaves stars

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Building Britain's Biggest Nuclear Power Station
programme.desc: Following the challenges involved with building the Hinkley Point C in the remote Somerset countryside, the first new nuclear power station in Britain for a generation. This time, the team create an extensive flood defence system to protect the site from a nuclear disaster like the one that befell the Fukushima Daiichi power plant in Japan. Elsewhere, cameras follow specialists as they work to uncover unexploded Second World War bombs that threaten the installation of the 4500-tonne cooling water intake heads

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett hosts as temperatures drop and winter hits the sewing room, with challenges themed around keeping warm and adding sparkle to the coldest months of the year. Patrick and Esme's Pattern Challenge this week is a man's flannel shirt. The trickiest pattern challenge yet, the sewers must create cuffs and collars, challenging both their construction technique and precise sewing skills

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey through East Anglia, where he discovers the Essex origins of the BBC and joins the Women's Land Army to pick damsons at Tiptree

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan bring the latest dramas from the live nest cameras in Norfolk. Iolo Williams heads to Loch Fleet on Scotland's east coast, hoping to catch ospreys and otters out fishing. Gillian Burke checks out the batty nightlife at WWT Castle Espie, and Megan McCubbin joins conservationists shaping the UK of tomorrow

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Return to Pembrokeshire Farm
programme.desc: Having restored the main farmhouse, Griff Rhys Jones embarks on the second phase of his Pembrokeshire project, turning his attention to the watermill and the derelict miller's cottage. With his son George, who is training to be an architect, overseeing the design, Griff plans to convert both these outbuildings into accommodation

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a sky hovering over snow-covered mountains and glass-like reflections

programme.title: Yes Minister
programme.desc: Cabinet reshuffle rumours and possible redundancy from high office anger Jim Hacker, who considers accepting an offer to become an EEC commissioner. Comedy, starring Paul Eddington

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: DeVere neglects to invite Audrey to the annual Hunt Ball - so she plans a socially acceptable revenge. Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles star

programme.title: Ian Hislop's Olden Days
programme.desc: The 1851 census revealed that Britain had become the world's first urbanised nation, an epochal change that ushered in an idealised vision of the countryside that has been celebrated by some British writers, painters and musicians ever since. Ian Hislop examines this rose-tinted view of the past in works by influential Victorian watercolourist Myles Birket Foster, whose paintings feature thatched cottages and apple-cheeked maids, and author JRR Tolkien, who portrayed the battle between the rural and the industrial in the fantasy world of The Hobbit and The Lord of the Rings

programme.title: Extra Life: A Short History of Living Longer
programme.desc: David Olusoga and Steven Johnson consider how, during a health crisis, the readiness of communities to engage with - and act upon - advice can have a significant impact on the spread of disease

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Mathematician Hannah Fry is joined by a selection of BBC science experts to investigate the British public's questions about the future, trying to discover whether people could live for ever and if there will be a cure for cancer. Hannah examines the future of transport and if it will be possible to ditch fossil fuels, looks at what the weather will be like and discovers if the planet is on the verge of another mass extinction

programme.title: Ocean Giants
programme.desc: Stephen Fry narrates this documentary exploring the vocal ranges of marine mammals. Sperm whales produce clicks to locate giant squid two miles beneath the ocean surface, and narwhals use a similar technique to find breathing holes in Arctic ice floes. Bottlenose dolphins communicate with fishermen in Brazil, humpbacks offer haunting operatic performances and boto dolphins create bat-like ultrasonic calls to help find fish in the murky Amazon River

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a sky hovering over snow-covered mountains and glass-like reflections

programme.title: Return to Pembrokeshire Farm
programme.desc: Having restored the main farmhouse, Griff Rhys Jones embarks on the second phase of his Pembrokeshire project, turning his attention to the watermill and the derelict miller's cottage. With his son George, who is training to be an architect, overseeing the design, Griff plans to convert both these outbuildings into accommodation

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: As an outbreak of headlice sweeps the school, Julia finds herself accused of triggering a second wave. Ostracised by the other mums, she needs to find a way back into their good books and decides to throw a nit treatment party at her home. The gathering reveals that Anne has some big news, Meg is facing a crisis, Kevin has committed a terrible crime of passion and Liz is waiting to hear about a possible career move. Comedy, starring Anna Maxwell Martin and Diane Morgan

programme.title: Ian Hislop's Olden Days
programme.desc: The 1851 census revealed that Britain had become the world's first urbanised nation, an epochal change that ushered in an idealised vision of the countryside that has been celebrated by some British writers, painters and musicians ever since. Ian Hislop examines this rose-tinted view of the past in works by influential Victorian watercolourist Myles Birket Foster, whose paintings feature thatched cottages and apple-cheeked maids, and author JRR Tolkien, who portrayed the battle between the rural and the industrial in the fantasy world of The Hobbit and The Lord of the Rings

programme.title: Curtain Up on Coventry
programme.desc: Coventry's streets are transformed into a giant stage as it finally gets to celebrate the start of a Covid-delayed year as UK City of Culture 2021

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a quiet little pond, hidden far from the nearest roadway in a densely covered spot

programme.title: Skies Above Britain
programme.desc: Two aspiring air traffic controllers begin the rigorous three-year training course, while an amateur pilot prepares for her first solo flight at Elstree Airfield, London. A resident of a once-quiet village in Kent leads a campaign to stop the narrowing of flight paths and increased noise overhead

programme.title: Coventry Cathedral: Building for a New Britain
programme.desc: In November 1940, German bombers destroyed much of the city centre of Coventry and reduced the city's medieval cathedral to rubble. This documentary tells the story of the new cathedral, consecrated on the same site in May 1962 and incorporating the ruins of the old building. Narrated by Pauline Black

programme.title: Everything Is Connected - George Eliot's Life
programme.desc: In this Arena programme, contemporary artist Gillian Wearing celebrates the legacy of Victorian novelist George Eliot. Just as Eliot's novel Middlemarch explored the lives of ordinary men and women, this experimental film is made up of a diverse cast of people from different backgrounds, and features Jason Isaacs and Sheila Atim as the narrators

programme.title: Great British Photography Challenge
programme.desc: The search for an exciting new name in British photography continues. This week sees the six contenders challenged to capture the character of flowers in rural Herefordshire, the adrenalin of urban free running and impress Rankin with a brand advertising assignment

programme.title: The Man Who Shot Tutankhamun
programme.desc: Margaret Mountford travels to Egypt's Valley of the Kings to investigate the story of British photographer Harry Burton, whose images of the Tutankhamun excavation in the 1920s created a global sensation. In addition to exploring the locations where Burton worked, the presenter also reveals how the images led to a craze in Egyptian designs, and how present-day photographers, such as Harry Cory Wright, are still influenced by his boundary-pushing work today

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a quiet little pond, hidden far from the nearest roadway in a densely covered spot

programme.title: Curtain Up on Coventry
programme.desc: Coventry's streets are transformed into a giant stage as it finally gets to celebrate the start of a Covid-delayed year as UK City of Culture 2021

programme.title: Coventry Cathedral: Building for a New Britain
programme.desc: In November 1940, German bombers destroyed much of the city centre of Coventry and reduced the city's medieval cathedral to rubble. This documentary tells the story of the new cathedral, consecrated on the same site in May 1962 and incorporating the ruins of the old building. Narrated by Pauline Black

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Live coverage from Florence, where the third meeting of the 2021 Diamond League is taking place. With the Olympics little more than a month away, some of the world's top athletes will be in Tuscany to test out their form, as well as accruing valuable points in the race to be crowned Diamond League champion. Colombia's Olympic triple jump gold medallist Caterine Ibarguen will be among those competing, and there's a clash of champions in the long jump, where reigning world outdoor champion Malaika Mihambo faces current world indoor champion Ivana Spanovic, who also won Olympic bronze in Rio in 2016

programme.title: A Town Like Alice (1956)
programme.desc: Second World War PoW Jean Paget is sent on a gruelling forced march between Japanese labour camps, but finds comfort in her friendship with Australian captive Joe Harman. During the journey Joe is caught stealing, tied to a cross and left to die. After the conflict is over, Jean is startled to receive a letter postmarked in Joe's home town of Alice Springs. Jack Lee's drama, based on the novel by Nevil Shute, starring Virginia McKenna, Peter Finch, Takagi and Jean Anderson

programme.title: Coventry Cathedral: Building for a New Britain
programme.desc: In November 1940, German bombers destroyed much of the city centre of Coventry and reduced the city's medieval cathedral to rubble. This documentary tells the story of the new cathedral, consecrated on the same site in May 1962 and incorporating the ruins of the old building. Narrated by Pauline Black

programme.title: How to Build a Cathedral
programme.desc: Architectural historian Jon Cannon explores how cathedrals were constructed, looking at fan vaulting at Gloucester, stained glass at York, and the Octagon at Ely for examples of medieval ingenuity, showing how they were built to realise a vision of Heaven on Earth

programme.title: Architects of the Divine: The First Gothic Age
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the development of the Perpendicular style, which took Britain away from the French-inspired buildings of the medieval age. The buildings examined include Gloucester Cathedral, which was built to commemorate a murdered monarch, and King's College Chapel in Cambridge, one of the last buildings to be constructed in the style. Presented by Nina Ramirez

programme.desc: It's Hank's 13th birthday and he wants the greatest party of all time. Unfortunately Stan and Rosa have other ideas, so Hank decides to have a secret party of his own in the den. Nothing can go wrong with that - right?

programme.desc: When Hank gets a B-plus at school thanks to even more of Papa Pete's support he realises he owes his grandad big time and gets the idea to find him a new girlfriend. programme.desc: The monsters try to flee when their Godmonster announces her return to check on their status as 'real monsters'. Oscar and Hedgehog help them out by proving that they are legitimately terrifying, but must rescue Oscar when Godmonster kidnaps him to cook him

programme.title: Dragons: Riders of Berk
programme.desc: The gang attempt to free a trapped scauldron dragon but have a race against time to do so before a pack of changewing dragons get to it.

programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Malaika is a wildlife film-maker and conservationist. Growing up in India inspired her to want to save the wildlife on her doorstep. She has always been fascinated by elephants and her mission is to investigate why these beautiful animals are is in such danger. The Indian elephant is classified as endangered, and Malaika learns that the biggest threats they face are forest fragmentation and living in captivity. She goes undercover to find out more about elephants in the tourist trade in Indian cities and then visits an elephant hospital run by Wildlife SOS, a charity which rehabilitates elephants rescued from captivity. Some of the elephants here were in chains for over 50 years and now have a chance to enjoy life in the wild. Malaika talks about the importance of being aware of wildlife tourism and encourages us to say no to exploitative elephant rides available on holiday.

programme.desc: Environmentalist Jack has made films since he was very young and has a loyal following online. More recently he has thrown himself into climate change activism and working to increase awareness of the threats to our planet. His mission is to investigate the growing but sometimes controversial movement to rewild Britain. Jack discovers how the first legalised reintroduction of the beaver to England is having dramatic results. It is re-engineering river systems and creating rich habitats for many other species. He learns that despite the benefits of having this giant rodent around, some people think bringing it back is bad news. Jack travels to the Highlands of Scotland to witness the beauty of a 300-acre area of land that has been allowed to regenerate in its natural way for the past 40 years. It is here that he comes face to face with endangered wildlife such as red squirrels and pine martens, that have crept back from the brink of extinction to live in this wilderness. Hed also learns

He has uprooted trees in order to make room for his Grimdome, but without the roots of trees to hold the soil together the heavy rainfall starts a mudslide down the side of the hill! programme.title: GiggleQuiz
programme.desc: Arthur Sleep is joined by team captains Gail Force and Keith Fitt in this slapstick quiz show featuring guests and clips from Gigglebiz. Joining Gail and Keith for this GiggleQuiz are Wizard Tripwick and Captain Adorable. Round 1 features clips starring Gail Force at an archeological dig and the Berrito Brothers testing their muscles with a strength-o-meter. At the end of Round 1, Arthur accidentally drops the Berrito Brother’s massive hammer onto Keith’s buzzer, smashing it into lots of pieces. Always on hand to save the day, Captain Adorable offers to fix the broken buzzer, but after some very unorthodox methods, Keith’s buzzer still isn’t buzzing properly. Round 2 is Nana Knickerbocker’s Panto Showdown, this time featuring Nana’s bees searching for her missing keys that are lost in gloopy, sticky honey. With Round 3 about to begin, Keith decides to take matters into his own hands and attempts to fix the broken buzzer himself. After watching a clip featuring DIY Dan gluing some tiles

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Callum is snappy with Ben, insisting he is fine despite being anything but - and things only get worse when Whitney and Kat ask him about the stabbing. Teddy bear repair duo Amanda and Julie welcome a 100-year-old RAF mascot bear called Bobby

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The three finalists take part in their biggest professional assignment to date, delivering their own masterclass in which they demonstrate a signature look in front of a panel of industry heavyweights. The next day the trio face their final creative brief, coming up with a look that demonstrates what makes them unstoppable as a make-up artist, before the judges decide which two will compete in the final face off. programme.desc: In Ellesmere Port, James wants to turn his neglected garden into a Cornish coastal haven, providing a safe space for his dog and visiting family. Enter Charlie Dimmock and Lee Burkhill with competing designs. Which one will James decide to go with?

programme.title: Wales: The Big Kick Off
programme.desc: Gabby Logan and Nathan Blake present this curtain-raiser to the Euro 2020 tournament, ahead of the national side's first game against Switzerland. They are joined in the studio by current and former Wales internationals to reminisce about the heroics of 2016 and get their take on this competition, and check in on the squad from their training camp in Cardiff. Plus, the Alarm perform the official Euro 2020 single, The Red Wall of Cymru

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Former wheelchair racer Richie Powell investigates the sport's classification system, asking whether something designed to make competition between athletes with different disabilities fair is flawed, easily manipulated and lacking credibility

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett hosts the semi-final, as the four remaining sewers go back in time to the 1940s. Attempting to revive both the glamorous and thrifty styles of the decade, they tackle the decade's most elegant and innovative garments. First up, judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young challenge the sewers to make the decade's most famous trousers, Oxford bags. For the Transformation Challenge, the sewers are set a wartime task to turn old parachutes into glamorous dresses. In the Made to Measure challenge, the sewers take on their final icon of 40s fashion, Dior's New Look. Characterised by an hourglass silhouette and involving masses of fabric, the sewers must exaggerate their model's figure to create the iconic shape

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Bethan Rhys Roberts presents the weekly show featuring stories and in-depth interviews about what is happening in Wales and why it matters

programme.title: A Question of Sport

programme.title: Football, Racism and Social Media
programme.desc: A look at the four days when the footballing world, with support from across sport, used its voice by staying silent on social media. The boycott was the latest attempt at tackling the racist abuse suffered by black and Asian players. Yet even those at the centre of the campaign conceded little would change as a result. Nesta McGregor speaks to victims, social media companies and those charged with cleaning up the sport to investigate why the abuse is happening and what can be done to stop it

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell track the progress of terraced houses in Grassmoor near Chesterfield, Queenborough in Sheerness, Kent, and St Helens on Merseyside

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: The Sheriffs are tasked with removing squatters from a shop in north London. James and Mark are in Birmingham hoping to takeaway money apparently owed by a kebab shop, and the Sheriffs visit a garage that allegedly damaged the wheels of a customer's pride and joy

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a single glove uncovered the identities of a prolific and violent gang, and an image of a distinctive ponytail that solved a high-tech bank heist

programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Sid plans an ambitious initiative for the Mill, and Al is less than helpful in Bear's audit meeting. Zara is forced to intervene between a mother and her stroppy teenage daughter

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz in which contestants aim to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest. Battling it out today are Sam, Sharna, Gary and Sandy

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps two brothers search for a house in Herefordshire. The duo have pooled their resources to have £800,000 to find the right country home where they can live together. Away from the hunt, Alistair is given an exclusive invitation to experience the best in British agriculture at the Royal Three Counties Show

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Ben and Shelley's new-build garden in Wing, Buckinghamshire, is sloped, impractical and has a tiny patio. Having travelled extensively across Europe, the couple yearn for a Mediterranean courtyard-style garden to remind them of their adventures abroad. It must also feature a children's play zone for youngsters Eva and Maya. Siblings David and Harry Rich Brothers vie with Arit Anderson to help the pair transform this troublesome space

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Izzie Balmer hit the shops of Staffordshire and Shropshire. Izzie's new obsession turns out to be serpentine in nature as she gets her hands on a couple of snake-related items. For Phil, there's a rather special bit of jewellery from a big-name designer that he has his sights on

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the magazine show, featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bobby arrives at the restaurant for another date with Dana, only for Iqra to drop him in it by suggesting that he should cook them dinner. Rainie tells Vi to go back home - only to be caught off guard when the old busybody lets her in on a secret. Nancy encourages Mick to speak up about Linda's plan to get in touch with Max about the baby. Isaac continues digging for information on Paul and Callum digs himself deeper as Ben makes a discovery

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies are joined by former Dragon Theo Paphitis, who standing in for a self-isolating Peter Jones. The Dragons are served a selection of premium spiced rums by a confident drinks entrepreneur. A nursing bra designed to help mothers and babies breastfeed effectively and a heatless hair curling device invented in a garden shed are also offered up, while a Midlands-based duo pitch a voice-enhanced app for learner-readers. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Launch Show
programme.desc: Gary Lineker and guests look ahead to this summer's European Championship, which begins in Rome with the curtain-raiser between Turkey and Italy. The tournament, which was delayed by a year because of the coronavirus pandemic, will take place in 11 different cities across the continent, with the semi-finals and final staged at London's Wembley Stadium. England have only ever won one knockout tie at the Euros, but hopes are high Gareth Southgate's side can improve that record. The Three Lions, who boast a formidable array of attacking options, begin their campaign against Croatia on Sunday. Scotland meet the Czech Republic in their opener, while Wales face Switzerland. Gary is joined by former England defender Micah Richards, ex-Scotland star James McFadden and Ashley Williams, who captained Wales at Euro 2016

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Our Cops in the North
programme.desc: A fire that kills a man and could have led to more deaths appears to be arson and the culprit is likely to strike again - but identifying the criminal proves tricky as all the evidence was destroyed in the blaze. Meanwhile, a neighbourhood team gathers enough intelligence to raid the address of a local suspected drug dealer, and paramedics are prevented from carrying out CPR

programme.desc: Izzie Balmer and Phil Serrell go on the hunt for more elusive antique treasures. A gamble on some brown furniture may prove profitable at the Hertfordshire auction

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Having chased Kat's mugger, Callum blames himself when the man gets away by attacking Fitzy, and cannot open up to Ben about what happened. Kat is reluctant to take Phil up on his business offer, not wanting to get involved in anything dodgy. Ruby and Lily reach and agreement and Stuart is appalled when Vi tries to set him up with Honey

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Callum is snappy with Ben, insisting he is fine despite being anything but - and things only get worse when Whitney and Kat ask him about the stabbing. Rainie is suspicious of Vi's sudden nice act, forcing Stuart to admit she knows about their surrogacy plans, Lola worries about Isaac when he lies about his medication, and Linda realises she will have to tell Max about the baby

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Kian discovers that Lucky betrayed him by calling social services, and as plans begin to move Andrei away, a desperate and powerless Kian spirals out of control. Ange and Josh are questioned about their secret relationship by Hanssen, leaving them worrying about their future at Holby, and Dom feels rejected when a patient requests Sacha to be her consultant

programme.title: Mr Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Former Scotland manager Craig Brown, along with players from his World Cup squad, remember France 98 - Scotland's last appearance at a major tournament until Euro 2020. Squad members including Colin Hendry, John Collins, Jim Leighton and Craig Burley are transported back into the midst of their French adventure, and that famous opening match with Ronaldo's Brazil at the Stade de France

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The three finalists take part in their biggest professional assignment to date, delivering their own masterclass in which they demonstrate a signature look in front of a panel of industry heavyweights. One contestant tackles male grooming, the second showcases special effects and the third aims to dazzle with a quick smokey eye. The next day the trio face their final creative brief, coming up with a look that demonstrates what makes them unstoppable as a make-up artist, before the judges decide which two will compete in the final face off. Hosted by Maya Jama

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: Lockdown is finally lifted, and in Bridgend Scott gets to open his doughnuts and coffee cafe. Cardiff nightclub owner Panikos auditions Russian cook Shev for the role of head chef, while vintage clothing entrepreneur and keyboard player Nanial prepares for his band to launch their first album

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: The Psycho prequel series returns as Norma sets out in search of the missing Norman. Meanwhile, Dylan awaits news of Emma, and Romero receives a surprising proposition. Thriller, starring Freddie Highmore and Vera Farmiga

programme.desc: Norma and Norman find themselves at odds, as each suspects the other of committing horrible actions. Meanwhile, Dylan considers his future with Emma, and Romero makes a decision that will have a big impact on the Bates family. Thriller, starring Vera Farmiga and Freddie Highmore
